content.problemStatement:
You are given a table with NNN rows and MMM columns. Let's denote the cell in row rrr and column ccc by (r,c)(r, c)(r,c). You are also given QQQ rectangles in this table.

Consider a directed acyclic graph such that each vertex corresponds to a cell of the table (so there are N‚ãÖMN \cdot MN‚ãÖM vertices) and there is an edge from cell (r1,c1)(r_1, c_1)(r1‚Äã,c1‚Äã) to cell (r2,c2)(r_2, c_2)(r2‚Äã,c2‚Äã) if and only if:


r1<r2,c1<c2r_1 < r_2, c_1 < c_2r1‚Äã<r2‚Äã,c1‚Äã<c2‚Äã
there is a rectangle containing cells (r1,c1)(r_1, c_1)(r1‚Äã,c1‚Äã) and (r2,c2)(r_2, c_2)(r2‚Äã,c2‚Äã)
Let's define the length of a path as the number of vertices it contains. Find the length of the longest path and the number of different paths with maximum length in this graph. Two paths are different if their lengths are different or there is a cell which is visited in one path, but not in the other. Since the number of paths can be large, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: the sum of N‚ãÖMN \cdot MN‚ãÖM for all test cases does not exceed 10710^7107
the sum of QQQ for all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105
the sum of QQQ does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
the sum of N‚ãÖMN \cdot MN‚ãÖM does not exceed 4‚ãÖ1054 \cdot 10^54‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and QQQ.
Each of the following QQQ lines contains four space-separated integers r1r_1r1‚Äã, c1c_1c1‚Äã, r2r_2r2‚Äã and c2c_2c2‚Äã describing a rectangle which contains all cells (r,c)(r, c)(r,c) such that r1‚â§r‚â§r2r_1 \le r \le r_2r1‚Äã‚â§r‚â§r2‚Äã and c1‚â§c‚â§c2c_1 \le c \le c_2c1‚Äã‚â§c‚â§c2‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing two space-separated integers ‚Äî the length of the longest path in the graph and the number of paths with this length modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are four edges in this graph: (1,1)‚Üí(2,2)(1, 1) \rightarrow (2, 2)(1,1)‚Üí(2,2) due to the first rectangle and (2,2)‚Üí(3,3)(2, 2) \rightarrow (3, 3)(2,2)‚Üí(3,3), (2,2)‚Üí(3,4)(2, 2) \rightarrow (3, 4)(2,2)‚Üí(3,4), (2,3)‚Üí(3,4)(2, 3) \rightarrow (3, 4)(2,3)‚Üí(3,4) due to the second rectangle.
There are no paths with lengths greater than 333 and two paths with length 333: (1,1)‚Üí(2,2)‚Üí(3,3)(1, 1) \rightarrow (2, 2) \rightarrow (3, 3)(1,1)‚Üí(2,2)‚Üí(3,3) and (1,1)‚Üí(2,2)‚Üí(3,4)(1, 1) \rightarrow (2, 2) \rightarrow (3, 4)(1,1)‚Üí(2,2)‚Üí(3,4).
